title: Name and date to appear as "time stamp" in long text to update process
excerpt: Hi everyone! I am looking for an integration that automatically adds the date, time and the user’s name every time they make a change in a long text column. For example to describe sales process progression, or latest up…

posts.author: DanaWD (Dana Wizman Danan)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi everyone! I am looking for an integration that automatically adds the date, time and the user’s name every time they make a change in a long text column. For example to describe sales process progression, or latest update about a property visited. Does this animal exist?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have? And what’s your use case, if there’s not, maybe we can help come up with a different idea?

posts.author: DanaWD (Dana Wizman Danan)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Desiree. I am using CRM Work Management and have the enterprise level. One use case, for example, is this: we’re working with lots of properties. every month or so we do a viewing to show it to potential guests. We have a column in our Property Board where we leave important feedback about the actual property (not the viewing) post viewing. If something changed, deteriorated or improved, or anything worth mentioning. I would want this (long text) column to be automatically add date, time and user leaving the update in the column. The “Last Updated” column in generic to any change that happens for a certain item so I cannot relay on this one as I am looking for a time stamp for specific column. I guess I can add a special date column that only updates if there is a change in a text column. I just wondered if the “stamp” can appear IN the long text column by default. Thanks in advance!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Got it, I’d recommend setting a separate date column and then setting automations based on that update only. You could possibly even use a text column with a button to add the note to the ‘updates’. Then have the date column just pull when the button was clicked.

posts.author: DanaWD (Dana Wizman Danan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks Desiree, for the idea. I never used “create update” automation. I appreciate your time and support

Dana

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s one I use all the time, hope it helps!
